message:
fix(ondisk,journal): propagate dir-block parse errors and reject malformed revoke blocks during replay

- Change lookup_in_dir_block and lookup_in_dir_block_casefold to return
  Result<Option<...>, ParseError> instead of silently swallowing parse
  errors via .ok()? — callers in ffs-core and Ext4ImageReader now
  propagate corrupt directory block errors instead of treating them as
  "entry not found"
- Replace parse_revoke_entries (which clamps oversized r_count to
  block.len()) with strict_revoke_entries during replay, causing replay
  to stop scanning when it encounters a revoke block with an invalid
  r_count — this prevents a transaction with a corrupt revoke block from
  being committed
- Mark the lenient parse_revoke_entries as #[cfg(test)] only
- Add jbd2_writer_open_stops_at_malformed_revoke_tail test verifying
  that Jbd2Writer::open stops scanning before a corrupt revoke block
- Update adversarial_revoke_r_count_exceeds_block_size test expectations
  to match the new strict behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
